editableNewDialogue.text: i’ve been thinking about how some songs feel totally different depending on whether you watch a live stage or just listen with headphones

Absolutely—live stages add visuals, crowd energy, and little vocal variations that can change the whole impression. Headphones tend to highlight production details like layering, reverb, and backing vocals. Which do you usually want to notice more: performance details or production details?
production details mostly, like hearing small synths or ad-libs

In that case, a fun trick is listening at low volume first (it makes the loudest elements stand out), then one repeat at normal volume to catch the “hidden” textures. Also, some platforms normalize loudness differently, so the same track can feel more compressed depending on where you play it.
true, i’ve noticed spotify vs youtube can sound different

Yep—different encodes and loudness targets can change clarity and punch. For example, YouTube typically uses Opus (in many cases) and Spotify often uses Ogg Vorbis/AAC depending on device and settings, and normalization can alter perceived dynamics. If you name one track, I can suggest what to listen for—like kick transient, vocal air, or stereo width.
also random: i read that the voyager golden record has music on it, which is kind of wild

It really is—Voyager’s Golden Record includes greetings in many languages plus a curated set of music (like Bach and Chuck Berry) meant as a snapshot of Earth. Totally different context than modern streaming, but it’s a neat reminder that “how” music is delivered can matter almost as much as the music itself.
since you like dreamcatcher’s rock-pop/edm side, do you get more into their title tracks or the b-sides?
mostly the b-sides tbh, i end up looping them more than the title tracks
i gotta correct something from before—during the 2020 lockdown i wasn’t really listening to k-pop groups. i mostly got into dreamcatcher only, not a bunch of groups.
Got you—so it wasn’t “K-pop groups” in general during COVID, it was mainly Dreamcatcher you were listening to through lockdown.
yeah exactly, dreamcatcher carried that time for me, but i didn’t really branch out to other groups.
